description: Fix four high-severity security vulnerabilities: IDOR on group endpoints, unvalidated settlement/split recipients, hardcoded SECRET_KEY, and DEBUG=true default

userStories.title: Add IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission class
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable DRF permission class that checks group membership so all group-scoped views can use it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission class in backend/core/permissions.py
Permission extracts group pk from URL kwargs and fetches the Group
Returns True if request.user is in group.members.all() OR request.user.is_staff
Returns False (403) for non-members who are not staff
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Apply group membership check to GroupExpenseView
userStories.description: As a user, I want my group's expenses to be private so only members and admins can access them.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupExpenseView in backend/core/views.py uses IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission
GET /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
POST /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
Staff users can still access any group's expenses (200)
Group members can still access their group's expenses normally (200)
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Apply group membership check to GroupSettlementView
userStories.description: As a user, I want my group's settlements to be private so only members and admins can access them.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupSettlementView in backend/core/views.py uses IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission
GET /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
POST /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
Staff users can still access any group's settlements (200)
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Apply group membership check to GroupBalanceView and GroupExportView
userStories.description: As a user, I want my group's balances and exports to be private so only members and admins can access them.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupBalanceView in backend/core/views.py uses IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission
GroupExportView in backend/core/views.py uses IsGroupMemberOrAdmin permission
GET /api/groups/{id}/balances/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
GET /api/groups/{id}/export/ returns 403 for authenticated non-member non-staff user
Staff users can still access any group's balances and exports (200)
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Validate settlement payee is a group member
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to ensure settlement payees are group members so users cannot create false debt records for arbitrary users.
acceptanceCriteria: GroupSettlementView.post() or SettlementSerializer validates that payee is in group.members.all()
POST /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ with a payee who is NOT a group member returns 400 with descriptive error
POST /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ with a valid group member payee still succeeds (201)
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Validate expense split user_ids are group members
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to ensure custom expense splits only reference group members so users cannot assign debts to arbitrary users.
acceptanceCriteria: ExpenseSerializer._create_splits() or ExpenseSerializer.validate() checks each user_id in split_data is in group.members.all()
POST /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ with split_data containing a non-member user_id returns 400 with descriptive error
POST /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ with valid group member user_ids still succeeds (201)
Equal splits (no split_data) still work correctly — splits created only for group members
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Remove hardcoded SECRET_KEY and default DEBUG to false
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want secure defaults so production deployments don't use an insecure secret key or expose debug pages.
acceptanceCriteria: settings.py reads DJANGO_SECRET_KEY with no insecure hardcoded fallback
If DJANGO_SECRET_KEY is not set, app raises a clear error on startup (unless running tests)
settings.py defaults DJANGO_DEBUG to 'false' when env var is not set
docker-compose.yml sets DJANGO_SECRET_KEY to a unique value (not the old insecure default)
docker-compose.yml sets DJANGO_DEBUG=false (or removes it to use safe default)
docker-compose.ghcr.yml updated similarly if it exists
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core

userStories.title: Security authorization tests — non-member access blocked
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests proving unauthorized group access is blocked so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/core/tests/test_security.py
Create a third test user (bob) who is NOT a member of the test group
Test: bob GET /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ returns 403
Test: bob POST /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ returns 403
Test: bob GET /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ returns 403
Test: bob POST /api/groups/{id}/settlements/ returns 403
Test: bob GET /api/groups/{id}/balances/ returns 403
Test: bob GET /api/groups/{id}/export/ returns 403
Test: staff (admin) GET /api/groups/{id}/expenses/ returns 200 (even if not member)
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core.tests.test_security

userStories.title: Security validation tests — invalid payee and splits rejected
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests proving invalid settlement payees and expense splits are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to backend/core/tests/test_security.py
Test: POST settlement with payee who is not a group member returns 400
Test: POST expense with split_data containing non-member user_id returns 400
Test: POST settlement with valid group member payee returns 201
Test: POST expense with valid group member split_data returns 201
Tests pass: cd backend && python manage.py test core.tests.test_security
